_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning how connecting words — including time words like 'then' and conditional words like 'if' — help us link and sequence ideas in texts.

_source.w: We are learning how connecting words — including time words like 'then' and conditional words like 'if' — help us link and sequence ideas in texts.
y: Connectives are the links that hold our writing together, making it flow logically.
Using a range of connectives makes our writing more sophisticated and easier to follow.
Understanding connectives helps us follow the logic of texts we read.
eg: We identify temporal connectives in a recount — 'first', 'then', 'after', 'finally'.
We use conditional words like 'if', 'unless' and 'when' to show conditions in our writing.
We highlight connectives in a text and discuss what relationship each one creates.
We rewrite a paragraph with better connectives to improve its flow.
We sort connectives into groups — time, cause, contrast, condition.
We practise using connectives to join ideas within and between paragraphs.
